comparison_with_2_distribution_face_base.interpretation: Asian passport-style ID photos are the detector ideal case (frontal, well-lit, occlusion-free, large central face). Detection rate is 100% (vs ~55% on the mixed reference cohort), and SCRFD score median is +0.07 higher (0.86 vs 0.79). This bounds the upper end of what the 2c face-detection track is asked to do; in-the-wild Asian faces (still TODO) will sit somewhere between this upper bound and the mixed reference cohort.

detector_determinism.note: Same 119 byte-identical photos uploaded into a second project via the wo_raw recognition path also produced 119 face detections; pairing by photo hash and comparing SCRFD score, the maximum absolute difference is 0 -- SCRFD on ORT CPU is fully deterministic on this workload.

meta.cohort_description: real CNNC employee ID photos (Asian-face passport-style, frontal, well-lit, single subject)

milestone_label: Asian face detection-rate / bbox / score baseline on real CNNC employee ID photos

next_steps.why: closes the upper-bound gap left by this passport-style cohort; will give a realistic detection-rate floor for SCRFD on Asian faces under varied conditions.

next_steps.gating_milestone: #5-bootstrap (>=50 corrections gate) -> feeds into #6 MobileNetV3 retrain
next_steps.why: high-confidence YOLOv8 misfires on collars/badges break naive score thresholding; cannot be ignored when ID photos enter the wo_raw pipeline.

commands: # Then re-run the validation_queries above against bundled-pg.
reproducer.prereq: Path A (#2a-real, commit ee62f38) + reverse smoke (#2a-real-reverse, commit 664afc3) already executed; both projects populated.

side_findings.finding: photos.width and photos.height are NULL for all 119 rows on both projects (person upload + wo_raw upload). Image dimensions are not being populated by the upload handler.
side_findings.fix_owner: server upload handler (Rust) -- decode dims via `image` crate and persist on insert
side_findings.impact: bbox geometry can only be reported in absolute pixels, not normalized fractions. Affects evaluation tooling that wants normalized bbox area or position.

side_findings.finding: Photos uploaded with owner_type=person on the bootstrap path produce ZERO tool/device detections (src project: tool_count=0|119, device_count=0|119). The same byte-identical photos uploaded as owner_type=wo_raw produce 214 tool detections. Person bootstrap path runs SCRFD only.
side_findings.impact: Confirms by design: bootstrap is face-only -- saves CPU on the gallery seeding leg. Worth documenting in docs/recognition_pipeline.md as part of #2a-person-bootstrap branching behavior.

status_note: Controlled passport-style ID-photo cohort only (n=119). Captures detection rate, SCRFD score distribution, bbox geometry, kps coverage, embedding pipeline coverage, and detector determinism on real Asian faces. Does NOT yet cover in-the-wild Asian faces (different lighting, angles, partial occlusion) -- those require a separate cohort and remain out of scope for this slice.

class_id_blacklist_implication.recall_risk_on_real_worksite_tools: zero -- neither person nor tie are real worksite tool target classes

class_id_histogram_meaning.0: COCO class 0 (person) -- one per photo, 119 / 119 photos (1.0 / photo). The full-frame ID portrait itself.
class_id_histogram_meaning.27: COCO class 27 (tie) -- 95 / 119 photos (0.798 / photo). Quantifies the lanyard / employee badge / uniform collar hypothesis from the original finding text.

tool_false_positive_finding.cohort: same 119 ID photos through wo_raw path
tool_false_positive_finding.finding: YOLOv8 fires `target_type=tool` at 1.798 detections per photo on Asian ID photos (214/119), all unmatched. Per-photo rate is lower than #2-tool COCO baseline (3.26 / photo), but score quantiles are SIGNIFICANTLY HIGHER on these false positives (median 0.8906 vs reference 0.6681, p75 0.9116 vs reference 0.7456). Visually these are likely uniform collars / lanyards / employee badges getting classified as in-distribution objects (tie / cell phone / book) by COCO-trained YOLOv8 with high confidence. Implication: a naive score-threshold filter on tool detections will NOT be enough on this cohort -- tool detections need context-aware suppression or class-specific blacklists. Routes to #5-bootstrap (tool false-positive correction loop) and is a strong argument for #6 MobileNetV3 head retrained on F1 worksite tools. Quantification (added 2026-04-29 via #5-bootstrap-collector ed0a102): the 214 FPs split cleanly by COCO class_id into class_id=0 (person) 119 / 119 photos (1.0 / photo) and class_id=27 (tie) 95 / 119 photos (0.798 / photo). A {person, tie} class blacklist on the wo_raw tool detection path therefore clears 214 / 214 = 100 % of these FPs with zero recall risk on the actual #5-bootstrap target classes (real worksite tools); class 27 alone clears 44.4 %.

validation_queries: SELECT count(*), avg(score) FROM detections WHERE project_id='f06e30b8-8aeb-487a-b67c-59354442bb01' AND target_type='face';

SELECT face_count, count(*) FROM (SELECT p.id, count(d.id) FILTER (WHERE d.target_type='face') AS face_count FROM photos p LEFT JOIN detections d ON d.photo_id=p.id WHERE p.project_id='f06e30b8-8aeb-487a-b67c-59354442bb01' GROUP BY p.id) sub GROUP BY face_count ORDER BY face_count;

SELECT max(abs(s.score - d.score)) FROM detections s JOIN photos sp ON sp.id=s.photo_id JOIN photos dp ON dp.hash=sp.hash AND dp.project_id='f54d2a48-537c-4ab3-8f5b-826662465410' JOIN detections d ON d.photo_id=dp.id AND d.target_type='face' WHERE s.project_id='f06e30b8-8aeb-487a-b67c-59354442bb01' AND s.target_type='face';
